round1: Round 1 is done, and it separated the wheat from the chaff. Marc leads the pack, actually demonstrating some hockey knowledge, unlike the bottom feeders (Stephanie, Nickall, Robin, Jake, Jamie) who collectively whiffed on the Carolina, Rangers, and Blues series, proving that "upset" is just another word for "my pick sucked."
round2: Round 2 is in the books, and judging by these picks, most of you spent it in a coma. While Stephanie and Kiersten actually paid attention, the rest of the pool, especially Nickall, Nathan, and Glenda with their measly single points, clearly thought the Panthers were going to win and the Hurricanes weren't going home.
round3: Round 3 is done, and if you picked the Oilers, you're likely still mourning. Colorado's sweep made most of your picks look like a joke, while Jamie was the only one to actually nail a series with Tampa Bay in 6. For everyone else, maybe try looking at the standings next time.
round4: Colorado took the Cup in 6, a fact that only Stephanie, Benedict, Nickall, Jaclyn, and Robin managed to grasp. Everyone else was either close enough for a pat on the head, or, in the case of Ryan, Glenda, Nathan, and Kiersten, completely living in a fantasy land. Kiersten, Tampa in four? Ouch.
overall: The 2022 NHL playoffs pool has concluded, and in a truly underwhelming display of predictive prowess, Stephanie, Jaclyn, Robin, Benedict, and Nickall have collectively stumbled across the finish line with a paltry 15 points each, sharing the championship trophy like kindergarteners with a single participation ribbon. While Stephanie somehow managed to string together back-to-back 'championships' with this dismal score, Jaclyn broke a 12-year drought, and Robin, Benedict, and Nickall finally, after a combined 38 years of futility, got their names on the board – though 'board' here refers more to a bulletin board of shame than a winner's plaque. On the other end of the spectrum, perennial champion Nathan decided to take a year off from winning, joining Ryan and Kiersten in the exclusive 'Zero Points Club,' a feat of absolute failure that Kiersten, in particular, seems to be mastering with back-to-back last-place finishes. Even multi-time champions Marc and Derrick, stalwarts of this esteemed competition, barely scraped by with a pathetic 4 points, proving that even the greats can have an off-year, or perhaps, just completely forget how hockey works. Glenda, meanwhile, managed to distinguish herself by picking a single correct game, earning her a lonely 5 points and a spot just outside the true bottom feeders.
